Earlier this week, Union Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju travelled to Qatar to convey India’s condolences on behalf of the government and the people of India following the death of Sheikh Hamad, who passed away on July 12 at the age of 74.
Rijiju said Sheikh Hamad was a visionary leader who nurtured the historic India-Qatar friendship, which has since evolved into a strategic partnership.
India observed one day of national mourning on Monday as a mark of respect for the late former Amir.

As a mark of respect, the National Flag will be flown at half-mast across India on all buildings where it is regularly displayed, while official entertainment will remain suspended throughout the day. Prime Minister Narendra Modi expressed profound condolences on the passing of the Father Amir, describing him as a visionary statesman who transformed Qatar into a nation of remarkable development and prosperity. Meanwhile, Union Minister for Parliamentary Affairs and Minority Affairs Kiren Rijiju is expected to visit Qatar shortly to convey condolences on behalf of the Government of India and reaffirm India's solidarity with the leadership and people of Qatar during this period of national grief.","score":0.81133276,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://news.webindia123.com/news/articles/World/20260717/4475756.html","title":"PM Modi, Qatar Amir reaffirm to strengthen Strategic ... - News","content":"Modi conveyed his heartfelt condolences.
